topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the shared room had become a tangible pressure. Every creak of the floorboards, every distant siren, sent a jolt through my system. I kept glancing toward the desk, a frantic, darting motion, hoping to catch my roommate, Liam, in the act of reading. Tonight, the air felt thick, suffocating. I’d left my journal open on the bedside table, a secret repository of all my racing thoughts and burgeoning fears. When I returned from the library, the first thing I noticed was the slight misalignment of the pages. A few lines were subtly different, as if someone had paused, traced a finger across the ink, and then moved on. My stomach tightened into a hard knot, and I couldn't bring myself to look directly at him. When he finally spoke, his tone was casual, almost breezy, which only amplified the churning in my chest. "Hey, did you see that new episode?" he asked, completely oblivious to the silent alarm bells ringing inside me. I just nodded, my throat suddenly dry.

[story 2]

My hands were slick with a cold sweat as I crept into the room. The overhead light cast long, distorted shadows, making everything seem too large, too watchful. I stopped just inside the doorway, my breath catching high in my lungs. Liam was sprawled on his bed, headphones on, engrossed in something. But as I watched, a flicker of movement caught my eye—a hand hovering near my desk. My heart began to hammer against my ribs, a frantic drumbeat I was desperately trying to slow down. I took a tentative step forward, my sneakers silent on the carpet. When I finally managed to speak, my voice came out reedy and shaky. "Liam? What are you doing?" He pulled one headphone off, a look of mild surprise crossing his face. He glanced at the journal, then quickly covered it with a textbook. "Oh, this? Just… browsing," he said, his voice too smooth, too unconcerned. The sudden withdrawal of his attention left me feeling exposed, every nerve ending screaming for me to flee.

[story 3]

I spent the afternoon pacing the length of the room, unable to settle. The low hum of the refrigerator seemed deafening. My focus kept snapping back to the desk, where my diary lay open, a beacon of my vulnerability. Every time I turned my head, I felt a distinct prickling sensation on the back of my neck. It wasn't just worry; it was a deep, internal tremor that made my limbs feel heavy and unsteady. I watched Liam throughout the day, noting the subtle shifts in his demeanor. He seemed unusually quiet lately, his gaze lingering near my workspace whenever I was near. When I finally dared to approach him later that evening, my voice was barely above a whisper. "Did you… look at my journal?" The air seemed to freeze between us. Liam blinked slowly, a strange, unreadable expression settling on his features. He didn't deny it, nor did he confirm it. Instead, he just met my eyes, and for a split second, I felt a profound sense of dread settle deep in my core, a cold weight that made it hard to draw a full breath.
